V-M-082.purpose: Verify manual external MTProto proxy pool storage, delivery-mode switching, owner payload selection, redaction, audit logging, UI honesty, and automatic-mode preservation.

module-checks.check-1: Backend tests cover manual proxy validation, encryption, redaction, activation, disable, delivery-mode switching, and owner response shape.
module-checks.check-2: Regression tests prove automatic mode continues to call the current KrotPN assignment path and preserves existing assignment identities.
module-checks.check-3: Admin tests prove confirmation guards and audit-safe action records for create, update, activate, disable, and mode switch.
module-checks.check-4: Frontend smoke proves user/admin UI labels manual external proxies clearly and never promises unavailable telemetry.

verification-levels.module-local: Service and schema tests prove storage, selection, redaction, and generated Telegram link correctness.
verification-levels.wave-level: Admin and owner API tests prove auth, confirmation, mode switching, and automatic/manual branch behavior.
verification-levels.phase-level: Frontend/admin builds, Phase-80 smoke, XML/MyGRACE checks, and protected deploy/runtime guard prove the plan is release-safe.

scenarios.scenario-1: Admin creates a valid external proxy and the stored secret is encrypted while admin list responses show only a masked secret fingerprint.
scenarios.scenario-2: Admin activates one proxy and manual_external owner response returns server, port, secret, tg_link, browser link, source=manual_external, and telemetry_available=false.
scenarios.scenario-3: Switching back to automatic returns source=krotpn_auto and preserves the existing M-043 assignment id/server/secret unless explicit reissue is requested elsewhere.
scenarios.scenario-4: Disabling the active manual proxy makes owner response safe/degraded instead of returning a broken or empty link.
scenarios.scenario-5: Invalid host, invalid port, non-hex secret, missing confirmation, non-admin auth, or missing proxy id is rejected.
scenarios.scenario-6: Admin list/detail, audit records, logs, and smoke output must not include full manual secrets or complete Telegram links.
scenarios.scenario-7: Manual external mode must not touch KPprotoN runtime policies, SNI router config, promotion tag apply state, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, VPN runtime, or AWG config.

api-contracts.api-1: GET /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies returns redacted manual proxy summaries for admins only.
api-contracts.api-2: POST /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies validates and stores an encrypted external proxy secret.
api-contracts.api-3: PATCH /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies/{proxy_id} updates safe fields or replaces the secret without returning the old secret.
api-contracts.api-4: POST /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies/{proxy_id}/activate requires confirmation and selects exactly one active manual proxy.
api-contracts.api-5: POST /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies/{proxy_id}/disable requires confirmation and removes it from manual delivery.
api-contracts.api-6: GET /api/v1/admin/mtproto/delivery-mode returns delivery mode and active manual proxy summary.
api-contracts.api-7: PUT /api/v1/admin/mtproto/delivery-mode requires confirmation and switches automatic/manual_external mode.
api-contracts.api-8: GET /api/v1/mtproto/proxy returns source and telemetry_available fields in addition to the existing owner-safe proxy payload.

data-invariants.invariant-1: Default delivery mode is automatic so clean deploy behavior remains current KrotPN MTProto issuance.
data-invariants.invariant-2: At most one manual proxy is active for delivery at a time unless a later approved phase introduces per-user assignment.
data-invariants.invariant-3: Manual proxy secrets are stored encrypted and never included in admin summaries or audit details.
data-invariants.invariant-4: Manual external proxies do not create M-054/M-056/M-060 telemetry or abuse claims because KrotPN does not own their runtime.

commands.command: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/modules/M-082.xml docs/plans/Phase-80.xml docs/verification/V-M-082.xml

redaction-rules.rule-1: Admin summaries may include proxy id, name, server, port, status, priority, verified_at, updated_at, and secret fingerprint only.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Admin responses, audit records, logs, and smoke output must not include raw external proxy secret or full t.me/tg proxy links.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Owner responses may include raw secret and links only for authenticated user cabinet delivery.
redaction-rules.rule-4: Promotion tag values remain governed by M-059 and are not leaked by manual proxy APIs.

protected-surfaces.surface-1: install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, KPprotoN runtime, SNI router, VPN runtime, and AWG config remain unchanged.
protected-surfaces.surface-2: Existing automatic MTProto assignments, CTA SNI generation, and promotion-tag runtime application remain unchanged.
protected-surfaces.surface-3: Analytics and abuse modules may show telemetry unavailable for manual external delivery but must not fabricate external proxy stats.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-80 is additive and operator-controlled, but it changes schema, admin API, owner MTProto response shape, and two frontends; execution must include backend tests, frontend builds, static smokes, redaction checks, and protected runtime/deploy guards.

acceptance.item-1: Manual external MTProto proxy pool can be configured and activated by admins only.
acceptance.item-2: Users receive manual external proxy links only when manual_external mode is active.
acceptance.item-3: Automatic KrotPN MTProto mode remains the default and stays restorable.
acceptance.item-4: No telemetry, abuse, or promotion-tag promises are made for external proxies.
